questions.text: A public S3 bucket listing contains customer medical record filenames. What is the IMMEDIATE priority?

options.text: Rename the bucket and keep it public, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Block public access, preserve logs, and initiate breach assessment per policy
Delete logs to save storage costs, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Post an apology tweet before containment, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Exposure of health data triggers containment and regulated breach workflows.
questions.concept: Classify data, block public exposure, and trigger breach workflows for regulated data.

questions.text: DLP alerts on a spreadsheet labeled 'Internal' leaving via personal webmail. Best FIRST step?

options.text: Confirm classification, block transmission if policy requires, and interview user with HR guidance
Disable DLP to reduce noise, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Fire the user automatically, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
Publish the file on the intranet, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Validate sensitivity and policy, then respond proportionally with HR/legal as needed.
questions.concept: Classify data, block public exposure, and trigger breach workflows for regulated data.

questions.text: Git history contains AWS keys for a retired project. What reduces future sensitive exposure?

options.text: Make the repo private without rotation, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Rotate keys, purge secrets from history, and enable secret scanning on all repos
Ignore because the project is old, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Add a README saying 'do not use keys', without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Secrets in VCS remain reachable until rotated and removed; scanning prevents recurrence.
questions.concept: Classify data, block public exposure, and trigger breach workflows for regulated data.

questions.text: A researcher finds patient MRNs in a public analytics dashboard screenshot on LinkedIn. FIRST step?

options.text: Ignore because social media posts are outside the organization's control boundary, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Ask the researcher to delete their post before IT investigates the dashboard
Change dashboard colors so MRNs are harder to read in future screenshots
Take the dashboard offline, preserve access logs, and start incident/breach assessment

questions.explanation: Confirmed PHI exposure requires immediate containment and regulated breach evaluation.
questions.concept: Classify data, block public exposure, and trigger breach workflows for regulated data.

questions.text: Laptops are stolen from a locked office; drives use BitLocker with TPM+PIN. Which data state is MOST protected?

options.text: Printed documents in recycling, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Data in use while logged in and unlocked
Data at rest on disk while powered off
Data in transit inside the LAN only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Full-disk encryption protects at rest when off; logged-in sessions remain exposed.
questions.concept: Protect data at rest and in transit; weak TLS and unencrypted backups expand blast radius.

questions.text: A team proposes TLS 1.0 for legacy clients on an internet-facing API. What is the BEST security stance?

options.text: Terminate TLS at the user's browser only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Enable SSLv3 for compatibility, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Deprecate TLS 1.0/1.1 and document exceptions with compensating controls and timelines
Use self-signed certs without rotation, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Legacy TLS is broken; phased migration beats indefinite weak protocols on public APIs.
questions.concept: Protect data at rest and in transit; weak TLS and unencrypted backups expand blast radius.

questions.text: Database backups are copied to tape unencrypted for 'speed.' What risk should leadership accept if continuing?

options.text: Cloud backups are automatically encrypted at rest always, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
No risk if tapes stay in the server room, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Encryption slows restores so it is never needed, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Physical loss of tapes exposes all backup contents without key compromise

questions.explanation: Offline media theft bypasses network controls; encryption at rest for backups is standard.
questions.concept: Protect data at rest and in transit; weak TLS and unencrypted backups expand blast radius.

questions.text: Developers store TLS private keys in the application Git repo for 'deployment speed.' Fix?

options.text: Share the repo with all contractors to improve on-call coverage, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Move keys to a managed secrets store with rotation and remove keys from Git history
Use longer RSA keys in Git without changing storage or access controls, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Encrypt the Git repo password while keeping keys in the same repository tree, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Private keys in VCS are exposed to anyone with repo access; use HSM/KMS-backed secrets.
questions.concept: Protect data at rest and in transit; weak TLS and unencrypted backups expand blast radius.

questions.text: DLP blocks a nurse emailing treatment notes to a personal account for 'convenience.' Which outcome is correct?

options.text: Disable DLP for clinical staff, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Block stands; offer approved secure transfer methods
Forward blocked content to IT via CC
Allow if the user is senior, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: HIPAA-aligned environments require controlling PHI egress; approved tools replace personal email.
questions.concept: Control exfil channels (email, USB, GenAI paste) with tuned policies and approved tools.

questions.text: Endpoint DLP generates many false positives on source code zips. What tuning is BEST?

options.text: Alert on every file save, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Scope policies to labeled repositories and exfil channels, not all zip creation
Whitelist all developers permanently, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Turn off endpoint DLP entirely, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Effective DLP targets high-risk egress with context to limit alert fatigue.
questions.concept: Control exfil channels (email, USB, GenAI paste) with tuned policies and approved tools.

questions.text: A contractor pastes customer PII into a public ChatGPT session. Which combined control helps MOST?

options.text: Disable spelling checker, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Block all HTTPS, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Remove contractor laptops, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Browser DLP, acceptable-use policy, and enterprise AI gateway with logging

questions.explanation: GenAI paste is a new exfil path; technical and contractual controls together reduce risk.
questions.concept: Control exfil channels (email, USB, GenAI paste) with tuned policies and approved tools.

questions.text: Engineers paste production database connection strings into a public Slack channel. Response?

options.text: Delete the Slack workspace and recreate it weekly to limit historical exposure, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Trust that Slack encryption makes public channel secrets safe from attackers, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Rotate credentials, purge channel history per policy, and enable DLP for chat exfil patterns
Ask engineers to use code comments instead of chat for sharing secrets, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Secrets in chat are credential exposure; rotate and add preventive DLP controls.
questions.concept: Control exfil channels (email, USB, GenAI paste) with tuned policies and approved tools.

questions.text: USB storage is disabled, but staff use phone charging cables that mount as MTP devices. What should you add?

options.text: Remove antivirus to improve speed, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Device control policies blocking unauthorized portable storage classes
Allow all USB if labeled with asset tags
Disable only Wi-Fi instead, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Charging-only vs data-capable USB requires device control, not just drive letters.
questions.concept: Unknown USB and MTP devices are untrusted; analyze only in isolated sandboxes.

questions.text: A found USB drive is plugged into a SOC analyst machine 'to see who lost it.' What is the SAFE procedure?

options.text: Open on the analyst daily workstation, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Email the files to the whole team, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Insert into the domain controller to scan faster, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Analyze only in a dedicated malware sandbox VM disconnected from production

questions.explanation: Unknown USBs are bait; sandbox isolation prevents accidental infection.
questions.concept: Unknown USB and MTP devices are untrusted; analyze only in isolated sandboxes.

questions.text: Marketing requests USB giveaways with preloaded brochures. Security concern to raise?

options.text: HIPAA requires USB giveaways, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
USBs are always read-only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Brochures cannot contain malware, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Supply-chain integrity—USBs could be tampered before distribution

questions.explanation: Preloaded media is a supply-chain risk; sourced devices and hashing matter.
questions.concept: Unknown USB and MTP devices are untrusted; analyze only in isolated sandboxes.

questions.text: A conference hands out branded USB drives at your booth. Security recommendation?

options.text: Autorun the drive on a reception PC to display the marketing PDF quickly, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Store drives in the server room safe as backup media for nightly jobs, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Distribute drives to executives first because they have the latest patches, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Do not insert into corporate systems; destroy or scan only in an isolated lab machine

questions.explanation: Untrusted USB media is a common initial-access vector; never trust giveaway drives.
questions.concept: Unknown USB and MTP devices are untrusted; analyze only in isolated sandboxes.

questions.text: Ransomware encrypts production; backups exist but are on the same VLAN as servers. Likely failure mode?

options.text: Ransomware deletes or encrypts online-connected backup shares
Snapshots cannot be targeted, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Tape libraries on VLAN are immune without testing
Backups automatically decrypt files, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Immutable or offline backups are required; network-local copies often die with production.
questions.concept: Immutable, offline, and tested backups survive ransomware; co-located copies often fail.

questions.text: Restore test last ran three years ago. Which risk is MOST acute?

options.text: Backups may be corrupt or procedures untested when needed
RPO is automatically zero, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Encryption is impossible on tape, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Backups run too frequently, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Untested backups are a common disaster recovery gap.
questions.concept: Immutable, offline, and tested backups survive ransomware; co-located copies often fail.

questions.text: A cloud admin can delete production and backup vaults with one role. Best fix?

options.text: Store backups only on the admin laptop, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Separate backup admin role with MFA, immutability, and break-glass monitoring
Disable logging on backup API, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
One super-admin for speed, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Backup integrity requires separation of duties and immutability features.
questions.concept: Immutable, offline, and tested backups survive ransomware; co-located copies often fail.

questions.text: Immutable backups are enabled, but backup admin can delete vault locks. Best improvement?

options.text: Separate backup admin with MFA, dual control on lock changes, and alert on deletion APIs
Store backup credentials in the same wiki page as production admin passwords, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Use one cloud root account for production and backups to simplify billing, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable immutability during incidents so ransomware can be deleted faster, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Backup protection fails if the same identity can delete production and backups.
questions.concept: Immutable, offline, and tested backups survive ransomware; co-located copies often fail.
